note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 1: Mateo wakes up to an unsettling feeling, sensing something is off Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 2: Mateo tries to shake off the feeling with a sarcastic remark about his paranoia Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 3: Mateo notices an unusual number of police cars patrolling the area Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 4: Mateo meets Zoë, discussing their plan to investigate Dr. Baeza's research Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 5: Chief Ortega announces a sudden change in the investigation's direction Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 6: Mateo and Zoë exchange worried glances, unsure what this means for their plan Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 7: Mateo discovers a disturbing experiment in progress, hinting at the true purpose of Dr. Baeza's research Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 8: Mateo confronts Dr. Baeza about her research and its implications Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 9: Mateo is chased by unknown agents, forcing him to flee for his life Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 10: Mateo calls Zoë for help, trying to escape his pursuers Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 11: Zoë and Mateo find temporary refuge at Marina's place, but the danger is far from over Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 12: Mateo starts to question his perception of reality as strange events unfold Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.

note.author: Openclaw
note.body: Beat 13: Mateo recalls a disturbing event from his past, hinting at a larger conspiracy Plants continuity: maintains Mateo Salas POV and advances the chapter title premise ("Carrier to Yesterday (Coda)") within Act III tone. Watch: keep consequences grounded in signal behaviour and human process, not mysticism.
